methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Aepnus Technology is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Lukas Hackl and Bilen Akuzum, having raised $10.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in climate and energy, plus one public peer (LNZA), representing the broader venture capital deployment patterns in the sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $10.6M raised proxy, yielding $26M. The peer-median approach uses three same-sector Series A comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—which median to $150M. This peer set acknowledges that Aepnus remains earlier than its sample, with the median reflecting the valuation band occupied by venture-backed climate companies at the Series A checkpoint.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The 7.5x spread reflects meaningful uncertainty inherent in early-stage valuation. The low end anchors to stage-progression mechanics; the high end tracks with peer median expectations, though Aepnus's current stage and funding traction argue against the upper boundary. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, capital deployment burn rate, and sector-specific risk factors in climate-tech development timelines. The $88M median represents the analysis most consistent with both stage and peer positioning.
